content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I’m a developer based in India, and this is my first post here, so I figured I’d start with something I’ve been building in my free time: an open source npm package called narrative-form.
Quick disclaimer before anything else — this is a personal, after-hours project. It has nothing to do with my day job. I built it because I couldn’t stop thinking about it, not because anyone asked me to.
Here’s the idea in one line: what if signing up for something felt like writing a letter instead of filling out paperwork?
Most sign-up flows fall into one of two buckets. There’s the classic form — labeled boxes, a progress bar, a “Submit” button waiting at the bottom. And there’s the chatbot version, where the same fields get dressed up as a fake conversation, complete with a “typing…” bubble pretending someone’s on the other end.
narrative-form is neither of those.
Instead, the app types out a sentence fragment on screen, character by character, typewriter-style — something like “Hi, my name is ___” — and you fill in the blank right there, inline, as part of the sentence. Once you confirm it, that line settles into place above, and the next fragment starts typing itself out beneath it. Line by line, a little letter builds itself, written together by the app and the user. If you need to go back and change something, there’s a small pencil icon next to each completed line — tap it, edit in place, and nothing else on the page resets.
No progress bar. No steps. No fake chat partner. Just a sentence, finishing itself with your help.
I went through a few names before landing on this one — it started as narrative-signup, then react-narrative-form, before I settled on narrative-form as the clearest description of what it actually is: not a form, not a wizard, not a chatbot, but a narrative you complete.
It’s live now:

• npm: @viveksinghind/narrative-form-react

• GitHub: github.com/vivekSinghInd/narrative-form

• Docs & demo: viveksinghind.github.io/narrative-forms/

• LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/vivek-singh2984
I’m still early in this — currently working through some post-launch cleanup — but the core interaction already feels like the thing I set out to build. If you try it, I’d genuinely like to know what you think, and what breaks.
More posts on the technical side (how the typewriter mechanic works, why I chose a monorepo, the framework expansion plans) coming soon.
